---
title: "DVP-2"
author: "Muhammad Iqbal"
date: "2025-06-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r import}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(jsonlite)

# Transaction Data
trips2 <- read_csv("trips2.csv", 
  col_types = cols(
    transID            = col_character(),
    payCardID          = col_character(),
    payCardBank        = col_character(),
    payCardName        = col_character(),
    payCardSex         = col_character(),
    payCardBirthDate   = col_double(),
    corridorID         = col_double(),
    corridorName       = col_character(),
    direction          = col_double(),
    tapInStops         = col_character(),
    tapInStopsName     = col_character(),
    tapInStopsLat      = col_double(),
    tapInStopsLon      = col_double(),
    stopStartSeq       = col_double(),
    tapInTime          = col_datetime(),      # assumes ISO datetime
    tapOutStops        = col_character(),
    tapOutStopsName    = col_character(),
    tapOutStopsLat     = col_double(),
    tapOutStopsLon     = col_double(),
    stopEndSeq         = col_double(),
    tapOutTime         = col_datetime(),
    payAmount          = col_double(),
    tapIn_built_year   = col_character(),     # may be NA
    tapIn_notes        = col_character(),     # e.g. “RAMP…”
    tapOut_built_year  = col_character(),
    tapOut_notes       = col_character()
  )
)

colnames(trips2)
```

```{r }
head(trips2)
```

```{r}
halts <- bind_rows(
  trips2 %>%
    select(
      id            = tapInStops,
      name          = tapInStopsName,
      lat           = tapInStopsLat,
      lon           = tapInStopsLon,
      corridorID,
      corridorName,
      built_year    = tapIn_built_year,
      remarks       = tapIn_notes
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      corridor = paste0(corridorID, " : ", corridorName)
    ) %>%
    select(-corridorID, -corridorName),

  trips2 %>%
    select(
      id            = tapOutStops,
      name          = tapOutStopsName,
      lat           = tapOutStopsLat,
      lon           = tapOutStopsLon,
      corridorID,
      corridorName,
      built_year    = tapOut_built_year,
      remarks       = tapOut_notes
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      corridor = paste0(corridorID, " : ", corridorName)
    ) %>%
    select(-corridorID, -corridorName)
) %>%
  distinct(id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    built_year = parse_number(built_year) %>% as.integer(),
    remarks    = replace_na(remarks, "")
  )

halts
```





```{r}
edges <- trips2 %>%
  count(
    from   = tapInStopsName,
    to     = tapOutStopsName,
    name   = "weight"
  ) %>%
  filter(from != to) %>%
  mutate(
    from = as.character(from),
    to   = as.character(to)
  )

network <- list(
  nodes = halts,
  edges = edges
)

edges
```
```{r}
network
```
```{r}
# Write JSON for D3
write_json(network, "bus_stop_network.json", pretty = TRUE, auto_unbox = TRUE)
```


```{r}
ts_data <- trips2 %>%
  transmute(
    id       = tapInStopsName,
    date     = as_date(tapInTime),
    weekday  = if_else(wday(date) %in% 2:6, "Weekday", "Weekend")
  ) %>%
  count(id, date, weekday, name = "count") %>%
  arrange(id, date)

write_json(ts_data, "bus_stop_timeseries.json", pretty = TRUE, auto_unbox = TRUE)

ts_data
```
```{r}
pyr_data <- trips2 %>%
  transmute(
    id      = tapInStopsName,
    sex     = payCardSex,
    birth   = payCardBirthDate,    
    date    = as_date(tapInTime)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(birth), birth > 1900) %>%       # drop invalids
  mutate(
    age     = year(date) - birth,
    # define 10-year bins: “0–9”, “10–19”, … “80+”
    age_grp = cut(
      age,
      breaks = c(seq(0,80,by=10), Inf),
      labels = c(paste0(seq(0,70,by=10), "–", seq(9,79,by=10)), "80+"),
      right = FALSE
    )
  ) %>%
  count(id, age_grp, sex, name="pop") %>%
  arrange(id, age_grp, sex)

write_json(pyr_data, "bus_stop_population.json", pretty = TRUE, auto_unbox = TRUE)

pyr_data
```
```{r}
hourly_summary <- trips2 %>%
  mutate(
    date     = as_date(tapInTime),
    hour     = hour(tapInTime),
    dow      = wday(date, week_start = 1),              # 1 = Mon … 7 = Sun
    day_type = if_else(dow <= 5, "Weekday", "Weekend")
  ) %>%
  # count trips per stop × hour × day_type
  count(
    id       = tapInStops,
    hour,
    day_type,
    name     = "trips"
  ) %>%
  # if you want avg/IQR across days, then:
  group_by(id, hour, day_type) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_trips = mean(trips),
    lower     = quantile(trips, .25),
    upper     = quantile(trips, .75),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

write_json(
  hourly_summary,
  "bus_stop_hourly_summary.json",
  pretty     = TRUE,
  auto_unbox = TRUE
)

hourly_summary
```


```{r}

```
